channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran war hinders the flow of U.N. aid through the Gulf to communities in need
item.description: It's not just oil tankers stranded near the Strait of Hormuz. U.N. aid shipments are also hindered by the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded: It's not just oil tankers stranded near the Strait of Hormuz. U.N. aid shipments are also hindered by the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Greetings from Nyeri, Kenya, where grandmothers help coach the next generation
item.description: A group of grandmothers in central Kenya have formed a soccer team to keep fit and to give hope to a generation of teenagers — whom they sometimes outrun on the field.
item.content:encoded: A group of grandmothers in central Kenya have formed a soccer team to keep fit and to give hope to a generation of teenagers — whom they sometimes outrun on the field.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Igunza

item.title: The Fed to meet about interest rates. And, Sen. Mullin faces DHS confirmation hearing
item.description: The Federal Reserve is expected to hold the benchmark interest rate steady today amid economic uncertainty. And, Sen. Mullin faces a confirmation hearing to lead the Department of Homeland Security.
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Reserve is expected to hold the benchmark interest rate steady today amid economic uncertainty. And, Sen. Mullin faces a confirmation hearing to lead the Department of Homeland Security.

(Image credit: Annabelle Gordon)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: With strikes above and crackdowns on the ground, Iranians describe life under siege
item.description: A state-imposed internet blackout has obscured the reality of life in Iran as the war rages on. Those fleeing through neighboring countries share a rare glimpse of what life is like in Iran.
item.content:encoded: A state-imposed internet blackout has obscured the reality of life in Iran as the war rages on. Those fleeing through neighboring countries share a rare glimpse of what life is like in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Arezou Rezvani

item.title: Author Scott Anderson on Iran's power structure after death of top security official
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with journalist and author Scott Anderson about Iran's power structure after the killing of Ali Larijani, the head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with journalist and author Scott Anderson about Iran's power structure after the killing of Ali Larijani, the head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: War can't entirely eliminate Iran's nuclear program, the U.N. atomic energy chief says
item.description: International Atomic Energy Agency head Rafael Grossi said Iran's nuclear program is heavily damaged, "but the material will still be there and the enrichment capacities will be there."
item.content:encoded:
International Atomic Energy Agency head Rafael Grossi said Iran's nuclear program is heavily damaged, "but the material will still be there and the enrichment capacities will be there."

(Image credit: Satellite image (c) 2026 Vantor)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Cheap drones are reshaping modern warfare — and catching the U.S. off guard
item.description: As Operation Epic Fury enters its third week, relentless attacks by cheap Iranian drones are being fended off by multi-million-dollar U.S. interceptors. How long can the math hold up?
item.content:encoded: As Operation Epic Fury enters its third week, relentless attacks by cheap Iranian drones are being fended off by multi-million-dollar U.S. interceptors. How long can the math hold up?
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Iranians fleeing their country share rare glimpse of what life is like amid war, Trump floats idea of "taking" Cuba, the Federal Reserve is expected to hold its benchmark interest rate steady.
item.content:encoded: Iranians fleeing their country share rare glimpse of what life is like amid war, Trump floats idea of "taking" Cuba, the Federal Reserve is expected to hold its benchmark interest rate steady.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: At least 400 dead in drug rehabilitation center strike in Kabul, Afghan officials say
item.description: Afghanistan says Pakistan is to blame for a strike on a drug rehabilitation center in Kabul that killed at least 400 people, the deadliest attack since fighting began weeks ago.
item.content:encoded: Afghanistan says Pakistan is to blame for a strike on a drug rehabilitation center in Kabul that killed at least 400 people, the deadliest attack since fighting began weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: Once the airstrikes stop, Iran's nuclear threat leaves no easy endgame
item.description: Even after airstrikes end, Iran's nuclear threat looms and diplomacy may be too late.
item.content:encoded: Even after airstrikes end, Iran's nuclear threat looms and diplomacy may be too late.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen
